summary: Corrected project team labels, tightened project linkage to Airtable record ids, and moved study reads into the existing studies service with D1 cache fallback.

task.request: Fix linked Project dashboard records, especially Studies, using the existing studies service rather than adding another service file.

decisions.decision: Use Airtable record ids beginning rec as canonical project identifiers.
decisions.reason: The live API, D1 project cache and Airtable evidence showed that Project dashboard traversal must use Airtable record ids, not PID-style local identifiers.
The repository already has a bounded studies service. A separate study-record-routes file would duplicate responsibility and muddy the service catalogue.

decisions.decision: Bypass the router's legacy direct GET /api/studies handler from the Worker entrypoint.
decisions.reason: The direct handler was Airtable-only, checked too few linked-project field candidates, hid pagination weaknesses and could not fall back to D1.

decisions.decision: Expose study-load failure on the dashboard instead of rendering No studies yet.
decisions.reason: No studies yet must mean a successful empty response. It must not mask a 429, 503, bad link field or empty cache.

changes: Project cards now render the API team label rather than suppressing Home Office Biometrics.
Impact writes now default to a linked Project field and reject non-Airtable project ids.

studies.js now validates project ids, paginates Airtable list records, tolerates multiple linked-project field names, syncs successful Airtable reads into rops_studies_cache, and reads D1 before Airtable unless refresh=1 is used.
worker.js now routes /api/studies through the service layer before the legacy core router can use its direct handler.
worker.js now exposes /api/_diag/project-linked-records?project=rec... for project-linked study diagnostics.
project-dashboard.js now renders a clear studies error region when linked studies cannot be loaded.

observedChecksBeforeTraceUpdate: Worker CI still running at observation time

risks: Airtable is still rate-limited in preview. If D1 has no studies cached for the project, the dashboard will now show a study-load error rather than a false empty state.
Actual study records cannot be seeded safely without inspecting the Airtable Project Studies record ids and linked Project field values.
The legacy direct studies handler still exists inside core/router.js for fallback contexts, but Worker entrypoint now intercepts /api/studies before it is reached.
nextValidationTargets: Confirm all checks on the new trace head.
Confirm the dashboard either renders real linked studies or shows a study-load error with status and source details.
